.svg-inline--fa {
  box-sizing: content-box;
  display: var(--fa-display, inline-block);

}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xs {

}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xs {
  vertical-align: 0em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-sm {
  vertical-align: -0.0714285714em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-lg {
  vertical-align: -0.2em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xl {
  vertical-align: -0.25em;

}

.fa-layers-counter, .fa-layers-text {
  display: inline-block;
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
}

.fa-layers {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 1em;
  position: relative;
  text-align: center;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);

.fa-layers .svg-inline--fa {
  inset: 0;

  position: absolute;
  transform-origin: center center;

.fa-layers-text {
  left: 50%;
  top: 50%;

  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-counter {
  background-color: var(--fa-counter-background-color, #ff253a);
  border-radius: var(--fa-counter-border-radius, 1em);
  box-sizing: border-box;
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);

}

.fa-layers-bottom-right {

}

.fa-layers-bottom-left {

  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));

}

.fa-layers-top-right {
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);

  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));

.fa-layers-top-left {

}

.fa-xs {

  line-height: calc(1 / 12 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */

.fa-sm {

}

.fa-beat {

  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-bounce {
  animation-name: fa-bounce;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.28, 0.84, 0.42, 1));
}

.fa-fade {
  animation-name: fa-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-beat-fade {
  animation-name: fa-beat-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-flip {
  animation-name: fa-flip;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-shake {
  animation-name: fa-shake;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 2s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin-reverse {
  --fa-animation-direction: reverse;
}

.fa-pulse,
.fa-spin-pulse {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, steps(8));
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .fa-beat,
  .fa-bounce,
  .fa-fade,
  .fa-beat-fade,
  .fa-flip,
  .fa-pulse,
  .fa-shake,
  .fa-spin,
  .fa-spin-pulse {
    animation: none !important;
    transition: none !important;
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat {
  0%, 90% {
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  45% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-scale, 1.25));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-bounce {
  0% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  10% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-x, 1.1), var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-y, 0.9)) translateY(0);
  }
  30% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-x, 0.9), var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-y, 1.1)) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-height, -0.5em));
  }
  50% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-x, 1.05), var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-y, 0.95)) translateY(0);
  }
  57% {

  }
  64% {

  }
  100% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-fade {
  50% {

  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat-fade {
  0%, 100% {

  }
  50% {

}
@keyframes fa-flip {
  50% {

}
@keyframes fa-shake {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(-15deg);
  }
  4% {
    transform: rotate(15deg);
  }
  8%, 24% {
    transform: rotate(-18deg);
  }
  12%, 28% {
    transform: rotate(18deg);
  }
  16% {
    transform: rotate(-22deg);
  }
  20% {
    transform: rotate(22deg);
  }
  32% {
    transform: rotate(-12deg);
  }
  36% {
    transform: rotate(12deg);
  }
  40%, 100% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-spin {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  100% {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }

.fa-rotate-90 {

}

}

.fa-rotate-270 {

}

.fa-flip-horizontal {

}

.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(1, -1);
}

.fa-flip-both,

}

.fa-rotate-by {

  fill: var(--fa-primary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-secondary {

  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-primary {
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-secondary {
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-primary,
.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-secondary {
  fill: black;
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-inverse {
  fill: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.fa-stack {
  display: inline-block;

<h1 id="http-requests">HTTP requests<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#http-requests" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h1>
<p>The <code>Fetcher</code> class provides rapid and lightweight HTTP requests using the high-performance <code>curl_cffi</code> library with a lot of stealth capabilities.</p>
<div class="admonition success">
<p class="admonition-title">Prerequisites</p>
<ol>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html">Fetchers basics</a> page to understand what the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response object</a> is and which fetcher to use.</li>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/selection.html">Querying elements</a> page to understand how to find/extract elements from the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html#selector">Selector</a>/<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response</a> object.</li>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html">Main classes</a> page to know what properties/methods the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response</a> class is inheriting from the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html#selector">Selector</a> class.</li>
</ol>
</div>
<h2 id="basic-usage">Basic Usage<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#basic-usage" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>You have one primary way to import this Fetcher, which is the same for all fetchers.</p>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_0"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_0 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">scrapling.fetchers</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">Fetcher</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
Check out how to configure the parsing options <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#parser-configuration-in-all-fetchers">here</a><p></p>
<h3 id="shared-arguments">Shared arguments<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#shared-arguments" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p>All methods for making requests here share some arguments, so let's discuss them first.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>url</strong>: The targeted URL</li>
<li><strong>stealthy_headers</strong>: If enabled (default), it creates and adds real browser headers. It also sets a Google referer header.</li>
<li><strong>follow_redirects</strong>: Controls redirect behavior. <strong>Defaults to <code>"safe"</code></strong>, which follows redirects but rejects those targeting internal/private IPs (SSRF protection). Pass <code>True</code> to follow all redirects without restriction, or <code>False</code> to disable redirects entirely.</li>
<li><strong>timeout</strong>: The number of seconds to wait for each request to be finished. <strong>Defaults to 30 seconds</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>retries</strong>: The number of retries that the fetcher will do for failed requests. <strong>Defaults to three retries</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>retry_delay</strong>: Number of seconds to wait between retry attempts. <strong>Defaults to 1 second</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>impersonate</strong>: Impersonate specific browsers' TLS fingerprints. Accepts browser strings or a list of them like <code>"chrome110"</code>, <code>"firefox102"</code>, <code>"safari15_5"</code> to use specific versions or <code>"chrome"</code>, <code>"firefox"</code>, <code>"safari"</code>, <code>"edge"</code> to automatically use the latest version available. This makes your requests appear to come from real browsers at the TLS level. If you pass it a list of strings, it will choose a random one with each request. <strong>Defaults to the latest available Chrome version.</strong></li>
<li><strong>http3</strong>: Use HTTP/3 protocol for requests. <strong>Defaults to False</strong>. It might be problematic if used with <code>impersonate</code>.</li>
<li><strong>cookies</strong>: Cookies to use in the request. Can be a dictionary of <code>name→value</code> or a list of dictionaries.</li>
<li><strong>proxy</strong>: As the name implies, the proxy for this request is used to route all traffic (HTTP and HTTPS). The format accepted here is <code>http://username:password@localhost:8030</code>.</li>
<li><strong>proxy_auth</strong>: HTTP basic auth for proxy, tuple of (username, password).</li>
<li><strong>proxies</strong>: Dict of proxies to use. Format: <code>{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}</code>.</li>
<li><strong>proxy_rotator</strong>: A <code>ProxyRotator</code> instance for automatic proxy rotation. Cannot be combined with <code>proxy</code> or <code>proxies</code>.</li>
<li><strong>headers</strong>: Headers to include in the request. Can override any header generated by the <code>stealthy_headers</code> argument</li>
<li><strong>max_redirects</strong>: Maximum number of redirects. <strong>Defaults to 30</strong>, use -1 for unlimited.</li>
<li><strong>verify</strong>: Whether to verify HTTPS certificates. <strong>Defaults to True</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>cert</strong>: Tuple of (cert, key) filenames for the client certificate.</li>
<li><strong>selector_config</strong>: A dictionary of custom parsing arguments to be used when creating the final <code>Selector</code>/<code>Response</code> class.</li>
</ul>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Notes:</p>
<ol>
<li>The currently available browsers to impersonate are (<code>"edge"</code>, <code>"chrome"</code>, <code>"chrome_android"</code>, <code>"safari"</code>, <code>"safari_beta"</code>, <code>"safari_ios"</code>, <code>"safari_ios_beta"</code>, <code>"firefox"</code>, <code>"tor"</code>)<br></li>
<li>The available browsers to impersonate, along with their corresponding versions, are automatically displayed in the argument autocompletion and updated with each <code>curl_cffi</code> update.<br></li>
<li>If any of the arguments <code>impersonate</code> or <code>stealthy_headers</code> are enabled, the fetchers will automatically generate real browser headers that match the browser version used.</li>
</ol>
</div>
<p>Other than this, for further customization, you can pass any arguments that <code>curl_cffi</code> supports for any method if that method doesn't already support them.</p>
<h3 id="http-methods">HTTP Methods<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#http-methods" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>

<p>Examples are the best way to explain this:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Hence: <code>OPTIONS</code> and <code>HEAD</code> methods are not supported.</p>
</blockquote>

<h2 id="session-management">Session Management<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#session-management" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>For making multiple requests with the same configuration, use the <code>FetcherSession</code> class. It can be used in both synchronous and asynchronous code without issue; the class automatically detects and changes the session type, without requiring a different import.</p>
<p>The <code>FetcherSession</code> class can accept nearly all the arguments that the methods can take, which enables you to specify a config for the entire session and later choose a different config for one of the requests effortlessly, as you will see in the following examples.</p>

<p>The <code>Fetcher</code> class uses <code>FetcherSession</code> to create a temporary session with each request you make.</p>
<h3 id="session-benefits">Session Benefits<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#session-benefits" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>A lot faster</strong>: 10 times faster than creating a single session for each request</li>
<li><strong>Cookie persistence</strong>: Automatic cookie handling across requests</li>
<li><strong>Resource efficiency</strong>: Better memory and CPU usage for multiple requests</li>
<li><strong>Centralized configuration</strong>: Single place to manage request settings</li>
</ul>

<h2 id="when-to-use">When to Use<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/static.html#when-to-use" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>Use <code>Fetcher</code> when:</p>
<ul>
<li>Need rapid HTTP requests.</li>
<li>Want minimal overhead.</li>
<li>Don't need JavaScript execution (the website can be scraped through requests).</li>
<li>Need some stealth features (ex, the targeted website is using protection but doesn't use JavaScript challenges).</li>
</ul>
<p>Use <code>FetcherSession</code> when:</p>
<ul>
<li>Making multiple requests to the same or different sites.</li>
<li>Need to maintain cookies/authentication between requests.</li>
<li>Want connection pooling for better performance.</li>
<li>Require consistent configuration across requests.</li>
<li>Working with APIs that require a session state.</li>
</ul>
<p>Use other fetchers when:</p>
<ul>
<li>Need browser automation.</li>
<li>Need advanced anti-bot/stealth capabilities.</li>
<li>Need JavaScript support or interacting with dynamic content</li>
</ul>
